content:
Hey Slayers, we've got a quick beta branch hotfix for you today! There won't be a big beta patch this week, but you can expect the beta content, changes, and fixes to come to the main branch soon.

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Fixed occasional crash when upgrading a card at rest site
- Fixed crash that would sometimes occur when entering rooms
  - When transitioning between rooms, old assets were being freed immediately while the game was still rendering them on screen. This caused rare crashes to desktop, most often when entering events, shops, or campsites. The fix defers the actual freeing to the end of the frame, after rendering is complete.
- Fixed an issue with the Game Over screen after discovering multiple enemies while playing in Polish
- Fixed rare combat errors that could occur when re-entering combat
- Fixed softlock on run histories from older versions of the game
- **Not Yet** card can no longer be generated in combat by effects like Skill Potion
- Score is now correct when at Ascension 10

**Multiplayer:**
- **Colorful Philosophers** event can no longer be encountered if a player has only Ironclad unlocked
- Fixed curse mismatching when some players would take **Neow's Bones** relic
- Fixed state divergence when **Neow's Bones** relic would trigger multiple reward screens
- Fixed not being able to load into a Daily multiplayer run

Hey, Slayers! Casey here. This week's beta patch brings two major changes to how scoring works for the Daily Leaderboards.

First and foremost, **the leaderboards will now only show your friends' scores**. We'll add a way to view global scores later. In general, we don't really want to incorporate invasive anti-cheat protection into the game so it's sort of impossible for us to prevent people putting in the maximum score.

Secondly, no more magic numbers. While there's still points accrued for unlocking new cards and such, the score that's sent to the leaderboards and how it's sorted is based on **whether you won, how many badges you accrued, and how quickly you finished the run** (in that order). This should clarify why you did better on a Daily run compared to your friends.

Because the schema has changed, **leaderboards between main branch and beta branch will be separate going forward**. We'll eventually add more features like sorting and whatnot, but in the meantime, do let us know what you think of these changes! Apologies in advance for any bugs...

Now let's take a look at the rest of the updates, including updated art, some reworks and balance changes, a new Ironclad card, and new Neow relics (one of which may be very familiar to seasoned Slayers).

**CONTENT AND BALANCE:**

Hey everyone, Anthony here. This week I will be grouping balance and content changes together to better talk about how each section changes overall.

**Ancients:**
- Added new Neow relics:
  - Neow's Bones: "Upon pickup, gain 2 random Neow Relics. Add 1 random Curse to your Deck."
  - Phial Holster: "Upon pickup, gain 1 potion slot and procure 2 random potions."
  - Winged Boots: "You may ignore paths when choosing the next rooms to travel to 3 times."

We have three new Neow options you can choose from. I especially like adding more variety to Neow, so we will be looking to expand her set of relics even more over time.

**Ironclad:**
- Added new Ironclad card, **Not Yet**: "Rare - Skill - Cost 2 | Heal 10(13) HP. Exhaust."
- Deprecated **Grapple** card
- Changed **Colossus** card: rarity decreased from Rare -> Uncommon

This set of changes is directly aimed at improving Ironclad's survivability in a big way. **Colossus** moving to the Uncommon pool helps to lessen the need for Rare defensive options, and should also help Ironclad players that are missing out on Weak. To make room for an Uncommon Colossus, an existing Uncommon had to be cut. **Grapple** was too low impact (and redundant with Juggernaut) so it made the most sense to cut. **Not Yet** gives the Ironclad a much needed healing option.

**Silent:**
- Changed **Acrobatics** card: rarity increased from Common -> Uncommon
- Reworked **Follow Through** card: "Uncommon - Attack - Cost 1 | Deal 6(8) damage to ALL enemies. If the last card you played this turn was a Skill, apply 1(2) Weak to ALL enemies." -> "Common - Attack - Cost 1 | Deal 7(9) damage. If you have 5 or more other cards in your Hand, hits an additional time."
- Buffed **Memento Mori** card: base damage increased from 8(10) -> 9(11)
- Buffed **Speedster** card:
  - Damage increased from 1(2) -> 2
  - Upgrade changed from +1 damage -> Gains Innate

**Acrobatics** has consistently been a problem as a Common, completely dominating both win rates and pick rates. When compared to the other cards in its family: Skim, Parse, Glimmer, and Burning Pact, it stood out as the only Common. At Uncommon I expect it to still dominate pick rates, while giving me the breathing room to not overly nerf Sly cards, and in fact maybe even give Silent buffs. I will be watching to see how this change impacts her pool in particular this week. This is *still* an experiment, so it could be reverted if this change ultimately ends up being worse for the game.

**Regent:**
- Changed **Regalite** relic: now grants Block for *any* card created, not just Colorless cards

This change makes **Regalite** consistent with Arsenal, and gives more love to the Regent's Status-generating cards.

**Enemies:**
- Deprecated Tunneleing Twosome fight from the Hive
- Nerfed **Living Fog**: no longer increases the amount of bombs it spawns over time
- Nerfed **Skulking Colony**: HP decreased from 74(79) -> 70(75)
- Nerfed **Byrdonis**: HP decreased from 91(99)-94(99) -> 81(90)-84(90)
- Nerfed **Doormaker**:
  - Overall damage numbers nerfed and moves moved around to better align with the doormaker powers
  - No longer applies debuffs
  - Gains less strength

**Doormaker** maintains his troublesome powers, but gets *several* changes to make the fight more manageable and feel less oppressive. Additionally, I have started focusing on removing some of the spikiness from Act 1, with the long term plan to shift some of the difficulty from Act 1 -> Act 3. The **Skulking Colony** change is hopefully the last for a while, as the minor HP adjustment allows killing it one turn earlier at Ascension 8.

**ART:**

- Added portrait art for the following cards:
  - Adaptive Strike
  - Shadow Shield
  - Smokestack
  - Bad Luck
  - Outbreak
- Added new art for Nonupeipe Ancient background
- Added new art for the following Neow Ancient relics:
  - Hefty Tablet
  - Neow's Talisman
- Updated art for Osty in character select screen

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

- Players on the beta branch now submit their scores to a separate beta-only leaderboard
- Added a scrollbar to the Run History screen
- Badges now show up in Run History screen
- Cleaned up the screen when loading a multiplayer Daily Run so that it matches the standard Daily Run screen
- Daily Leaderboards now show off floor, badge count, and time instead of score
- In multiplayer games, gold score is averaged out across all players to make score uploading consistent for the new schema
- Updated Leaderboard view in Game Over screen
- Leaderboards shown in the Game Over screen in Dailies now fit better
- Your name on the Leaderboards are now highlighted blue so you can more easily find yourself
- Reworded **Spiraling Whirlpool** event to explicitly state that it can only enchant a basic Strike or Defend
- Added wrapping navigation to Relic Inventory bar on controller
- Big divination button hotkey in Crystal Sphere event no longer uses deck view button on controller

**WRITING:**

- **Archaic Tooth** relic now properly says "Transform a starter card *into* an Ancient version" instead of "*with* an Ancient version".
- **Flak Cannon** card description now highlights "Status" in gold
- **Flanking** card now properly specifies that it only doubles attack damage
- **Iteration**, **Rocket Punch**, and **Trash to Treasure** card descriptions now say "Status" instead of "Status card"
- **Pael's Tooth** relic now says "Cards devoured" instead of "Cards in the Pot"
- Updated translations for various languages
- Fixed translation errors for the following languages:
  - German
  - Japanese
  - Russian
- Updated credits

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Fixed a blackscreen-at-launch issue related to Steam crashing
- Fixed softlock related to opening a secret debug menu
- Fixed an issue where, if the player dragged a multi-target attack out of their hand, the damage amount previewed on the attack wouldn't include their Strength if some enemies had Vulnerable while others didn't
- "Ascension unlocked" text is no longer displayed in Daily/Custom runs
- Fixed leaderboards not being centered properly on non-standard aspect ratios
- Fixed cards in hand not being deselected when pressing the top panel hotkey button
- Fixed Merchant hand not initially hovering over the first card in the shop when on controller
- Players and enemies' maximum Block has been increased to 999,999,999
- Negative power amounts can no longer stack below -999,999,999

**Silent:**
- Fixed **Blade of Ink** card listing the generated Enchanted Shivs in the Run History combat rewards
- Fixed **Knife Trap** card causing the Exhaust Pile UI to display an incorrect number of cards

**Regent:**
- Fixed "to All enemies" not appearing in the **Sovereign Blade** Forge animation when playing **Seeking Edge** card
- Fixed **Reflect** card enchanted with Imbued wearing off before the player turn actually begins

**Necrobinder:**
- Fixed **Borrowed Time+** card not having green text in the upgrade preview
- Fixed self-Doom killing the player if they kill the enemy via **Stampede** card first

**Enemies:**
- Fixed **Knowledge Demon** background music not playing finale when you defeat him
- Fixed **Queen** background music not ending if you kill her before the Amalgam
- Fixed missing **Crossbow Ruby Raider** fire animation
- Poison now always procs before **The Insatiable** tries to eat you

**Events:**
- Fixed being able to drink the fruit potion in the **Stone of All Time** event if you open the popup as the screen is fading out

**Multiplayer:**
- Fixed being able to play potions in multiplayer when you are dead
- Fixed **Belt Buckle** relic removing Dexterity if another player gets a potion from **Alchemy** card
- Fixed **History Course** relic playing **Believe in You** card before the start-of-turn Energy reset
- Fixed **Smokestack** power proccing off of other player's Statuses
- Fixed strange behavior after exiting the timeline via a Steam Invite
- Fixed some state divergences when using console commands in combat
- Fixed some instances of "unexpected noconnection packet" error
- Fixed state divergence related to upgrading a **Monologue** card enchanted with Royally Approved
- Fixed timing-based softlock related to **Golden Compass** relic

Hey Slayers, we've got another Beta patch for you all this week!

Notably, this patch brings in some structural work for a system called Badges.

Badges may appear at the end of a run, noting various things that happened during a run like defeating a boss without losing HP, beating the game quickly, specific easter eggs, or pointing out that you forgot to spend your gold. They're meant as little reminders to let you know what was unique about each adventure.

While they only show up at the end of a run for now, they'll appear in your run history and stats screen later on. This is part of a series of upcoming changes to make runs feel distinct and improve scoring (more on that later!).

In terms of visual changes, there are some new art additions for various cards and a few enemies, as well as some cool new VFX for afflictions!

Up next, we've got some more commentary from Anthony on this patch's content reworks, so let's dive right into it.

**CONTENT:**

**General:**
- Reworked Ascension 6 from Gloom - "Less rest sites." -> Inflation - "Removing cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive."

As mentioned last week, affecting map generation through Ascension has increasingly seemed to be a miss. It's often too hard to tell what change if any is a result of Ascension, or if it was just an unlucky map generation. Instead Ascension 6 will now make card removal at the Merchant start at 100 Gold, and will increase in price by 50 gold per removal instead of 25. I expect this to be felt significantly more than the old Gloom modifier.

**Silent:**
- Reworked **Blade of Ink** card: "Rare - Cost 1 - Skill | This turn, whenever you play an Attack, gain 2(3) Strength this turn." -> "Rare - Cost 1 - Skill - Add 2(3) Inky Shivs into your Hand."
  - Added new Inky enchantment: "This card deals 2 additional damage and applies 1 Weak."

The Blade of Ink rework is an experiment with generating Enchanted cards mid combat. Definitely let us know how this feels or if Enchantments are best left to things like Events and Relics. I suspect this card will be high impact, and gives Silent another strong Weak applier.

**Necrobinder:**
- Reworked **Borrowed Time** card: "Uncommon - Cost 0 - Skill | Apply 3 Doom to yourself. Gain 1(2) Energy." -> "Uncommon - Cost 1 - Skill | Gain 4(6) energy. Cards cost an additional energy this turn."

This new Borrowed Time should go particularly well with high cost cards like Reap and Bury and help to flesh out other ways to play Necrobinder.

**Enemies:**
- Reworked **Doormaker** Grasp turn and power: now attacks for 20(23) and the power is reworked to "Whenever you play a card, lose 1 energy."

Doormaker's Grasp turn was too underwhelming, and overall lowered the difficulty of the fight too much. This new version should make the entire fight high stakes and actually give the player a difficult problem to puzzle over turn to turn instead of making the fight about just living until turn 4.

**BALANCE:**

**Silent:**
- Buffed **Leading Strike**:
  - Shivs increased from 1 -> 2
  - Damage decreased from 7 -> 4
- Nerfed **Speedster**: damage decreased from 2(3) -> 1(2)
- Nerfed **Untouchable**: Block decreased from 7(9) -> 6(8)

**Potions & Relics:**
- Changed **History Course** relic: when it plays a copy of an X-cost card, it now uses your current energy for its X-value instead of the energy spent the last time the card was played. Note: It still plays the copy for free

**Enemies:**
- Buffed **Skulking Colony**:
  - Now attacks on all turns
  - Deals slightly more damage

**Ancients:**
- Buffed Nonupeipe's **Fur Coat** relic: now affects restocked Axebots and other enemies that enter combat during a fight

**Events:**
- Nerfed **Hungry For Mushrooms** event: Fragrant Mushroom option upgraded cards decreased from 3 -> 2
- Changed **Waterlogged Scriptorium** event:
  - Prickly Sponge option gold cost lowered from 155 -> 99
  - Tentacle Quill gold cost lowered from 65 -> 55
- Buffed **Whispering Hollow** event: Exchange option gold cost decreased from 50 -> 35+-9

**ART:**

- Added portrait art for the following cards:
  - Hotfix
  - Modded
  - Synthesis
  - Sweeping Gaze
- Added new art for Ruby Raiders
- Added background art and VFX for Vantom boss fight
- Added new visuals for the following afflictions:
  - Bound
  - Entangled
  - Galvanized
  - Hexed
  - Ringing
  - Smog
- Updated empty chest VFX, smoke puffs

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

**General:**
- Adjusted and added placeholder assets for score lines in run summary and game over screen
- Adjusted timings and pauses for dialogue for Gremlin Merc
- Adjusted Cultist "Cacaw" dialogue timings
- Adjusted speech bubble positions for Damp and Calcified Cultists
- All monsters now have distinct speech bubble colors
- Players can now navigate between cards/potions/relics in the Timeline unlock screen on controller
- Players can now navigate to the top bar character portrait to see the Ascension hovertip on controller
- Pressing the top bar shortcut while already focusing on a top bar element now moves your controller focus back into your hand

**Multiplayer:**
- Better handling of intermittent Steam errors
- Disabled skipping chests in multiplayer. Too many people thought it was the Proceed button.

**WRITING:**

**General:**
- **Foul Potion** description now specifies that it deals damage to "ALL players and enemies" instead of "EVERYONE", making it clearer that it does not hit Osty
- Fixed wording in the Statistics Total Playtime/Fastest win hovertip

**Localization:**
- Updated translations for various languages
- Fixed translation errors in the following languages:
  - German
  - Japanese
  - Italian
  - Spanish
  - Thai
  - Polish

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Fixed broken Steam rich presence translations
- Fixed a crash that could occur on startup after a Steam Cloud sync conflict
- Fixed a crash that could occur when the game failed to start and tried to show an error dialog
- Fixed an issue where numbers were improperly formatted on the victory screen
- If there is an error while spawning a creature into combat, a placeholder is shown instead of softlocking the combat
- Fixed softlock caused by being able to use your controller to confirm a hand card selection while you are peeking
- Fixed being able to press down arrow to controller navigate the card grid while an upgrade/remove/transform preview is up
- Vulkan is now defaulted to on Intel iGPUs with known D3D12 stability issues

**Ironclad:**
- Fixed softlock when selecting a card to upgrade via **Armaments** card while **Lethality** power still applies

**Silent:**
- Fixed **Bullet Time** card getting played by **Mayhem** power not setting the cost of the cards in your hand to 0

**Potions & Relics:**
- Fixed **Unsettling Lamp** relic doubling the Strength you get from **Dominate** card
- If a potion is discarded just before combat ends, it's no longer forever unusable
- When clicking a potion that can be thrown at all enemies simultaneously, the potion popup now properly says "Throw" instead of "Drink"

**Enemies:**
- Fixed an issue where a few enemies' damage intents were occasionally showing the wrong number
- Fixed not being able to navigate from the relic inventory back to the combat in the **Mysterious Knight** fight on controller
- Removed broken text from **Thieving Hopper** enemy's Escape Artist power hovertip when playing in Polish
- Fixed **Soul Nexus** animation not playing correctly

**Ancients:**
- Removed the peek button from the card selection via Neow's **Hefty Tablet** relic
- Effects that occur before your turn now interact correctly with Vakuu's **Whispering Earring** relic
- Fixed Vakuu's **Whispering Earring** relic continuing to play cards after **Void Form** power ended the turn
- Fixed an issue where Vakuu's **Lord's Parasol** relic would occasionally only obtain some of the items that the Merchant sells
- Fixed being able to end the **Lord's Parasol** purchase animation on controller

**Events:**
- Fixed an issue where, if the player visited a combat-style event without actually starting a combat (i.e. choosing "Return the Key" at The Lantern Key event) and then visited the Merchant immediately after, throwing a Foul Potion at the Merchant would not grant the player any gold

**Multiplayer:**
- **Trash to Treasure** power no longer procs from other player's generated statuses
- Fixed softlock in **Thieving Hopper** fight if one person is dead when it tries to steal cards
- Fixed state divergence related to **Driftwood** and **Dream Catcher** relics
- Player name is now correctly displayed when hovering over your character
- When re-entering the multiplayer host screen, the Ascension is now correctly synced
- You can now reload after saving a game at a rewards screen with non-standard rewards

Hey everyone, Anthony here. It's time for another Beta Patch where we test out experimental changes. Today we are going to do something a little different for the patch notes as I will be giving some insights into our design intent and thoughts behind various changes. Reminder that nothing in the beta branch is set in stone, expect lots of back and forth, and above all please let me know what you think by using the in-game feedback tool by hitting F2! I read countless pieces of player feedback and it has been instrumental in helping guide our process.

**CONTENT:**

**Reworks and Reverts:**

**Silent:**
- Deprecated **Prepare** and **Prepared** returns: "Common - Skill - Cost 1 - "Discard 2 cards. Next turn, gain 2(3) Energy." -> "Common - Skill - Cost 0 - Draw 1(2) card(s). Discard 1(2) card(s)."

**Necrobinder:**
- Reverted (Buffed) **Capture Spirit** to its previous version: "Uncommon - Skill - Cost 1 - Enemy loses 2(3) HP. Add 2(3) Souls into your Draw Pile." -> "Uncommon - Skill - 1 - Enemy loses 3(4) HP. Add 3(4) Souls into your Draw Pile."
- Reverted (Buffed) **Borrowed Time** to its previous version: "Uncommon - Skill - Cost 0 - Apply 6(3) Doom to yourself. Gain 1 Energy." -> "Uncommon - Skill - Cost 0 - Apply 3 Doom to yourself. Gain 1(2) Energy."

Starting off, I am reverting the changes made last week to **Prepared**, **Borrowed Time**, and **Capture Spirit**. For Prepared, while I still think that the Sly synergies are overall too dominant of a strategy, Prepared is so integral to the Silent's core identity that I will be looking for a different approach to bring Sly in line in the future. Borrowed Time is likely to receive a complete rework in the future, but for now I prefer that the card is fun to play with in all decks.

**Regent:**
- Reworked **Arsenal** card: "Power - Rare - Cost 1 Whenever you play a Colorless Card, gain 1(2) Strength." -> "Power - Rare - Cost 1 (Innate.) Whenever you create a card, gain 1 Strength."

**Potions & Relics:**
- Reworked **Pendulum** relic: "Common - Whenever you shuffle your Draw Pile, draw a card." -> "Common - Every 3 turns, draw 1 card."

**Enemies:**
- Reworked and nerfed the **Doormaker**. Fight is now less random.

**Doormaker** is receiving another rework! Now each visual phase he is in has its own unique power to hamper the player. All of them are disruptive and will hopefully encourage interesting play, but should now be less random and reward player agency more.

**BALANCE:**

**General:**
- Elites can no longer spawn on floor 6 to better match StS1
- Improved map generation consistency
- Fixed maps sometimes generating with fewer shops than intended
- Gloom Ascension modifier no longer affects ? rooms in addition to Rest Sites

Since Neow now counts as the first floor of the map, Elites were actually spawning one floor earlier than they should have been. This change should have a big impact on how tough fighting early Elites is. Additionally we found some improvements could be made to the map generation algorithm to help limit the number of maps overflowing with early monster rooms and not much else. For the Gloom change, although it did make the game more difficult it was not in a satisfying or fun way as it just made maps less interesting. Instead I will be looking to rework Gloom entirely in the future to a modifier that makes the game harder but in a more interesting way.

**Ironclad:**
- Buffed **Cinder** card: now Exhausts a random card in your Hand instead of the top card of the Draw Pile
- Changed **Tremble** card: now Exhausts

Tremble gaining Exhaust is a buff to Ironclad's Exhaust synergies, and coupled with the Cinder change should have a large impact on the Ironclad's cardpool.

**Silent**
- Buffed **Serpent Form**: Damage increased from 4(5) -> 4(6)

**Regent:**
- Buffed **Falling Star** card: Damage increased from 7(11) -> 8(12)
- Buffed **Glitterstream** card: Next Turn Block increased from 4(6) -> 5(7)
- Buffed **Parry** card: Block gain increased from 8(11) -> 10(14)
- Buffed **Refine Blade** card: Forge increased from 6(10) -> 9(13)
- Buffed **Celestial Might** card: Upgrade changed from +2 damage -> 1 additional hit
- Buffed **Guiding Star** card: now draws cards immediately instead of next turn
- Buffed **Sword Sage** card: no longer increases the cost of Sovereign Blade
- Changed **Spoils of War** card: now draws 2 cards, but Forge decreased from 12(17) -> 5(8)

**Defect:**
- Nerfed **Voltaic** card: Energy cost increased from 2 -> 3

**Potions & Relics:**
- Buffed **Tiny Mailbox** relic:
  - Rarity moved from Common -> Uncommon
  - Potions procured increased from 1 -> 2
- Changed **Permafrost** relic: Rarity moved from Common -> Uncommon
- Changed **Bag of Marbles** relic: Rarity moved from Uncommon -> Common
- Changed **Red Mask rarity** relic: Rarity moved from Uncommon -> Common
- Changed **Bellows** relic: Rarity moved from Uncommon -> Rare
- Changed **Lasting Candy** relic: Rarity moved from Rare -> Uncommon
- Changed **Crossbow** Tanx relic: Now uses Free to Play instead of Costs 0 Energy
- Changed **Vexing Puzzlebox** relic: Now uses Free to Play instead of Costs 0 Energy (Now also only lasts until end of turn)
- Changed **Liquid Memories** potion: Now uses Free to Play instead of Costs 0 Energy
For relic rarity, the design goal is that commons are not necessarily weaker, but that they are often simple effects that could add value to most decks. As rarity increases we allow higher complexity and more impact on drafting/play decisions.

**Enemies:**
- Nerfed **Skulking Colony**: deals less damage, damage scales slower, and no longer applies Dazed status cards
- Changed **Haunted Ship**: now applies Dazed status cards to the player
- Nerfed **The Lost and Forgotten**: stat theft no longer scales with Ascension
- Nerfed **Waterfall Giant**: HP decreased from 250(260) -> 240(250)

Skulking Colony went from too weak to too strong. This nerf should put his encounter more in line with how difficult the fight was intended to be. Additionally I found that the Dazed statuses actually worked against the design goals of his power, the new version is a tighter design overall that presents a more unified question for the player to answer. If the encounter is now too weak, simple numbers can be buffed in the future.

**Events:**
- Disallowed Gold generating relics from showing up as the featured relic in the Welcome to Wongo's event
- Mad Science Skill Chaos option (In the Tinker Time event) now uses Free to Play instead of Costs 0 Energy

**ART:**

- Added Test Subject burn animation
- Axe and Crossbow Ruby Raiders now have improved textures
- Added Portrait art for the following cards:
  - Compact
  - Shatter
  - Fuel
  - Consuming Shadow
  - Subroutine
  - Trash to Treasure

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

- Date at top-right now reflects build date instead of current date
- Increased feedback form character limit from 500 to 8000

Receiving in-game feedback from YOU the player is the best way we have of improving the game. Many of you have requested the ability to write more without hitting the character cap too early. We've increased the character count 16 fold, so I am eagerly looking forward to reading your effort posts!

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Fixed exploit where you could controller navigate to card grid when the upgrade/remove/enchant/transform preview screen is open
- Fixed a crash that could occur at the start of the player's turn when abandoning a run
- Fixed a softlock that could occur when abandoning a run during a combat-style event
- Fixed cards upgraded to cost 0 no sorting correctly when ordering by cost in the deck view
- Fixed the Y and X glyphs being incorrect on Switch controllers
- Format large numbers on victory screen using the player's language
- Localize feedback screen Category label
- A proper error is displayed if you try to join an in-progress game
- Fixed softlock when a monster's visuals would fail to load
- Numbers now are formatted appropriately to the player's language

**Necrobinder:**
- Fixed Grave Warden+ showing Soul+ in its hovertip preview
- Fixed Seance+ showing Soul+ in its hovertip preview
- Foul potion no longer damages Osty

**Potions & Relics:**
- Fixed a state divergence related to Joss Paper relic
- Fixed a state divergence related to Dualcast card and Snecko Eye relic

**Enemies:**
- Fixed a softlock when playing Scrape card against The Doormaker boss
- Fixed blackscreen when loading a game saved at the rewards from the Mysterious Knight combat

**Ancients:**
- Fixed being able to save and load at Neow to avoid the damage taken from Precarious Shears
- Fixed softlock when you play an Imbued card that prompts a card selection and Vakuu's Whispering Earring relic
- Fix issues with Pael's Eye relic when the player dies at the start of the extra turn

**Events:**
- Fixed Doll Room SFX continuing to play if you save and quit out from the event
- Fixed state divergence when you use Driftwood relic at any event
- Fixed timing-related state divergence at the Doll Room event
- Fixed timing-related state divergence when entering Battleworn Dummy event
- Waterlogged Scriptorium event's Tentacle Quill and Prickly Sponge options now properly show a hovertip for Retain

**Multiplayer:**
- Fixed back button not reappearing if you unready in an Multiplayer load game lobby
- Fixed Beacon of Hope power hovertip description so it no longer says it stacks
- Bottled Potential now shuffles the target player's cards, not yours
- Fixed Radiate card counting Stars from other players
- If both players have Pael's Eye and only one player triggers the extra turn, the second player no longer takes their extra turn immediately afterward
- Fixed rare timing issue when any player selects Random
- Fixed Haunt proccing when another player plays Soul

Welcome to the first BIG post-launch patch, Slayers!! This one has a huge balance pass, the main aim of which is to make infinites harder to achieve. We're also introducing a Phobia Mode with bespoke assets that should mitigate various creepy phobia concerns. Speaking of visuals, here are some other major highlights: some snazzy new portrait art for various cards and cool new character-specific VFX for when you gain energy!

Reminder that **this patch is exclusive to the beta branch branch for now**, which you can access via the instructions outlined in the first beta patch notes. It will come to the main branch later, likely with some more tweaks!

**CONTENT:**

**General:**
- Added a **Deprecated Card** placeholder: Status - "Draw 1 card. Remove this from your Deck. Exhaust."
  - If you have an in-progress run and a card in your deck is removed from the game due to a recent update, it will be replaced with "Deprecated Card"

**Ironclad:**
- Reworked **Dominate** card: Skill - Cost 1 - Uncommon - "Apply 1(2) Vulnerable. Gain 1 Strength for each Vulnerable on the enemy. Exhaust."
- Reworked **Expect a Fight** card: Skill - Cost 2(1) - Uncommon - "Gain  for each Attack in your Hand. You cannot gain additional  this turn."
- Reworked **Spite** card: Cost 0 - Attack - Uncommon - "Deal 5 damage. If you lost HP this turn, hits 2(3) times."
- Reworked **Stoke** card: Skill - Cost 1 - Rare - "Exhaust your Hand. Add 1 random (Upgraded) card into your Hand for each card Exhausted."

**Silent:**

Hey, Slayers! Happy 1-week-since-launch day! Today's patch mostly contains a lot of bug fixes, but it also contains a few art additions and a new limit to max HP values.

Heads up that this patch will at first be exclusive to the **newly released public-beta branch** for players who want to try out the newest updates and ensure they're stable before they're pushed to the main branch. You can access it by right-clicking on *Slay the Spire 2* in your Steam Library, then clicking Properties > Game Versions & Betas > public-beta.

**BALANCE:**

- Players, enemies, and pets can no longer have their HP increased above 999,999,999

**ART:**

- Add icon for Murderous run modifier
- Added portrait art for the following cards:
  - Feeding Frenzy
  - Sloth
  - Waste Away

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Pressing B on controller while focused on a dropdown menu no longer also closes the settings screen
- Fixed crash when interacting with potions during combat transitions
- Fixed crash when syncing cloud saves on Windows
- Fixed rare save data loss on Windows when power is lost during a save
- Fixed crash when quickly navigating away from menus
- Fixed dev console failing to match commands on non-English system locales
- Fixed potential softlock when auto-played cards trigger grid or card selection
- Fixed state divergence related to discarding potions too soon after combat ends
- Fixed being able to "peek" in card screens outside of combat
- Fixed closing the settings menu while a dropdown is open not also closing the dropdown
- Fixed navigating down the dropdown items causing the settings to scroll down
- Fixed softlock when auto-played cards triggered card selection

**Ironclad:**
- Fixed **Hellraiser** power playing Strikes if there are no valid targets

**Potions & Relics**:
- Fixed **Fairy in a Bottle** and **Lizard Tail** relics not healing you when your Max HP is 1
- Fixed **Massive Scroll** relic description saying that it can only give you Colorless multiplayer cards
- Fixed softlock if you proc **Lasting Candy** relic from the fixed tutorial card rewards
- Fixed **Hand Drill** relic applying Vulnerable to the player if **Burn** card breaks their block

**Enemies:**
- Fixed missing **Kaiser Crab** boss death VFX
- Fixed **Kaiser Crab** face animation not looping after a claw takes damage
- Fixed the word "Stunned" not appearing when **Rock Bowlbug** does its stunned move

**Ancients:**
- Fixed Tezcatara's **Brightest Flame** card being able to decrease your Max HP to less than 1

**Events:**
- Fixed softlock when dying in the **Jungle Maze Adventure** event
- Fixed softlock if you die in the **Room Full of Cheese** event

**Multiplayer:**
- Fixed softlock when any player has **Potion Belt** and **Alchemical Coffer** relics
- Fixed being unable to navigate via controller if you unready yourself in lobby
- Fixed players being unable to end their turn when they die while the **Whispering Earring** relic autoplays their cards
- Fixed **Doormaker**'s Door HP not scaling correctly after the Doormaker escapes
- Fixed softlock when queueing a potion to be thrown after the enemy would die
- Fixed crash when returning to the map after loading a multiplayer save
- Fixed host and client sometimes generating different maps from the same seed
- Fixed map changing after reconnect with the **Big Game Hunter** modifier or **Treasure Map** and **Golden Compass** relics
- Fixed softlock caused by a player dying with cards in their hand
- Fixed state divergence related to playing Huddle Up during another player's card selection
- Fixed timing-related state divergence occurring at the end of a combat
- Fixed rare instances of getting stuck at an empty chest

**Localization:**
- Fixed crash when selecting Ironclad on Turkish locale systems
- Fixed Russian card descriptions showing raw template text for **Call of the Void**, **Coolheaded**, **Fuel**, and **Ice Lance** cards
- Fixed an issue when viewing run histories in Russian where the player played as The Defect
- Fixed an issue with **Buffer+** card's description when playing in Portuguese
- Fixed an issue with **Call of the Void** card's hovertips when playing in Castilian Spanish
- Fixed an issue with **Colossus** card's hovertips when playing in German
- Fixed an issue with the "Accept" option in **The Trial** event when playing in French
- Fixed an issue with the "Violence" option in the **Tinker Time** event when playing in Korean

**LOCALIZATION:**

- Added more translated text for various languages
- Fixed translation and formatting errors for the following languages:
  - Italian
  - Russian
  - Simplified Chinese
  - Turkish
  - German
  - Spanish
  - Polish
- Adjusted Japanese font to be rendered slightly smaller and raised to improve vertical centering
- Improved Korean font spacing
- Improved Russian and Thai font rendering
- Improved the look of Simplified Chinese font
- Share button in stats screen now utilizes correct localized string

**MODDING:**

- Reworked mod structure
  - mod_manifest.json has been moved outside of the PCK. All mods should now ship with an additional file named <mod_id>.json - see ModManifest for the new JSON structure.
  - PCK is no longer required for all mods. Json is the only file that is required to declare a mod. The json must declare whether the mod includes a pck/dll file.
  - settings.save now includes a list of all mods that were loaded in the last game run. Reordering this list affects load order.
  - Mods may declare dependencies in the manifest. If settings.save declares a load order incompatible with the listed dependencies, then a re-sort is forced.
  - Added affects_gameplay field to mod manifest. If false, then the mod will not be checked against the host's mods in a multiplayer game.
  - Updated 0Harmony/MonoMod to support mac arm64. Manually overwriting it should no longer be required.

It's the last weekly patch before Slay the Spire 2's Early Access launch!! Thank you to all of our playtesters who have helped get us to this point. Your feedback has been invaluable! These notes are mostly some slight balance adjustments, bug fixes, and visual polish. We're almost to the next stage of StS2's evolution!!

**CONTENT:**

- Achievements have been temporarily disabled for Early Access

**BALANCE:**

**Necrobinder:**
- Nerfed **Flatten** card: damage decreased from 14(18) -> 12(16)

**Potions & Relics:**
- Changed **Paper Krane** and **Tingsha** relics: rarities swapped
- Changed **Razor Tooth** relic: now only upgrades Attacks and Skills

**Enemies:**
- Buffed **Seapunk**:
  - Sea Kick move damage increased from 9(10) -> 11(12)
  - Strength gain now scales with Ascension from 1(1) -> 1(2)
- Nerfed **Test Subject**: Phase 3 Lacerate damage decreased from 11(12) -> 10(11)
- Changed **Thieving Hopper**: is now pickier about the cards he steals
  - He will only steal cards that are in your deck (meaning no cards generated in combat)
  - He will try NOT to steal any cards with the Imbued enchantment unless he has no other choices
  - His card rarity priority order is as follows:
    - Priority 1: Uncommon cards
    - Priority 2: Common, Rare, or Event cards
    - Priority 3: Basic or Quest cards
    - Priority 4: Ancient cards or cards with the Imbued enchantment
    - Priority 5: Anything else (in practice, this should only ever be Curses)

**Enemies:**
- Changed **Tinker Time** event: now randomly offers 2 out of the possible 3 options for card type and effect

**ART:**

- Added portrait art for Neow's Fury and Wish cards
- Added new Nibbit Hiss animation
- Added Living Shield attack animation
- Added Shiv throw animation
- Added Wormy Impact VFX
- Added Gaseous Screen VFX
- Improved the resolution of the characters in the shop
- Added Main Menu art for Bestiary, Relic Collection, Potion Lab, Join, Host, Load from save, and Abandon Run

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

General:
- Hovertips are now blocked while you are in targeting mode
- Players can now close combat hotkeys with the same hotkey they opened them with
- Prevented other hovertips from popping up while the potion selection hovertip is open

Multiplayer:
- Ascension icon fire is now blue in multiplayer
- Corrected remote mouse focus when remote player is using controller

**AUDIO:**

- Added Sewer Clam SFX
- Updated Main Menu background music to fade out in Timeline
- Updated Orb SFX

**WRITING:**

- Added more translations for various languages

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Fixed aspect ratio dropdown menu
- Exiting/re-entering Custom Run screen with a seed now correctly uses the seed
- Feedback screen now changes text when language is changed
- Fixed 16:10 aspect ratio messing up settings alignment
- Fixed mouse jumping to wrong position during screen transitions in windowed mode
- Fixed being able to start card targeting via numpad after combat ends
- Fixed Timeline scroll still dragging after player would unlock an Epoch
- If players go back to an event room after they already hit Proceed, the Proceed button is no longer disabled

**Necrobinder:**
- Fixed an issue where Melancholy card's cost reduction would persist between combats if the reduction was due to Osty dying

**Enemies:**
- Fixed an issue where Skulking Colony encounter was appearing in the Run History as Mad Crab
- Killing Doormaker with the kill all console command no longer causes a softlock
- Killing the last non-minion enemy with Thorns while it's attacking and summoning a minion in the same move (such as Fabricator) now properly ends combat

**Ancients:**
- Fixed some Yummy Cookie relic display bugs

**Events:**
- Fixed Crystal Sphere event card reward icons not showing the correct card rarity icon in the rewards screen

**Multiplayer:**
- Extra votes are no longer displayed when players are dead
- Fixed orange color getting stuck in multiplayer HP bars
- Hands in multiplayer treasure screen now correctly point at relics

This Thursday's patch includes some reworks, lots of balance changes, new art, and some Epoch additions and rewrites! Also this week we are **resetting saves** once again, so if you see an error that says "There was a problem loading your save," no need to submit a bug report.

**CONTENT:**

**General:**
- Set up new Epochs in the Timeline

**Necrobinder:**
- Reworked **Countdown** card
- Reworked **Undying Sigil** relic

**Regent:**
- Reworked **Sword Sage** card

**Ancients:**
- Reworked Neow's **Fishing Rod** relic
- Reworked **Neow's Fury** card
- Reworked Tezcatara's **Seal of Gold** relic
- Pael blessings are now pooled
- Tezcatara blessings are now pooled

**Events:**
- **The Lantern Key** is now a combat-room-style event

**BALANCE:**

**Silent:**
- Buffed **Ring of the Drake** relic: turns increased from 2 -> 3
- Nerfed **Abrasive** card: Thorns decreased from 5(8) -> 4(6)
- Changed **Reflex** card: rarity increased from Common -> Uncommon
- Changed **Ricochet** card: rarity decreased from Uncommon -> Common

**Defect:**
- Changed **Chill** card: upgrade changed from gain Innate -> lose Exhaust

- Nerfed **Power Through** card: Block decreased from 15(20) -> 13(17)
- Changed **Spinner** card: upgrade changed from gain Innate -> Channel 1 Glass on play
- Nerfed **Thunder** card: damage decreased from 8(11) -> 6(8)

**Necrobinder:**
- Nerfed **Capture Spirit** card: HP loss decreased from 6 -> 3(4)
- Changed **Forbidden Grimoire** card: upgrade changed from gain Retain -> decrease Energy cost by 1
- Nerfed **Oblivion** card: Doom decreased from 6(9) -> 3(4)
- Nerfed **Pull From Below** card: damage decreased from 7(9) -> 5(7)
- Nerfed **Sic Em** card: Summon decreased from 3(4) -> 2(3)
- Nerfed **Spirit of Ash** card: Block decreased from 4(6) -> 4(5)

**Regent:**
- Nerfed **Crescent Spear** card: base damage decreased from 8 -> 6
- Nerfed **Furnace** card: Forge decreased from 5(7) -> 4(6)
- Nerfed **Stardust** card: damage decreased from 6(8) -> 5(7)

**Enemies:**
- Buffed **Doormaker**:
  - Attack damage increased from 30(33) -> 35(40)
  - Gains 5 Strength on the turn that he escapes
- Changed **Haunted Ship**: now does its Haunt move on turn 2 instead of 3
- Changed **Toadpoles** normal encounter: swapped the position of the Cultist and the Toadpole
- Changed **Knights** encounter: swapped the position of the Spectral Knight and the Magi Knight
- Buffed **Infested Guardian**:
  - Bug Sting move damage increased from 10(12) -> 6(7)x2 damage
  - Bug Sting move debuffs increased from 1 -> 2 each

- Nerfed **Twig Slime**: HP decreased from 27(28)-29(30) -> 26(27)-28(29)
- Buffed **Living Fog**: turn 1 Gas move now also deals 8(9) damage
- Buffed **Slimed Berserker**: Pummeling move damage increased from 2(3)x4 -> 4(5)x4
- Buffed **Wasp Prism**:
  - Jab move damage increased from 20(21) -> 22(24)
  - Pulsate Block increased from 12  -> 18(20)
  - Radiate move damage and block increased from 14(16) -> 16(18)

**Ancients:**
- Changed **Neow**'s blessings: Scavenger (one random relic) and Large Capsule (two random relics for Strike+Defend) blessings are now exclusive from each other
- Buffed Vakuu's **Crimson Pendant** relic: Apparitions increased from 2 -> 3

**Events:**
- Changed **Crystal Sphere** event:
  - Payment Plan option Divinations increased from 5 -> 6
  - Uncover Future option Divinations decreased from 5 -> 3
- Nerfed **Self-Help Book** event: Nimble option's Nimble enchantment decreased from 3 -> 2

**Multiplayer:**
- Nerfed **Tank** card: no longer stacks

**ART:**

- Added portrait art for the following cards:
  - Uproar
  - Boost Away

  - Thunder
  - Disintegration

- Crab

  - Vakuu

- Added Sweeping Beam VFX
- When epoch slots can be clicked the effect is now purple, bigger, and layered above other epoch slots

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

- Boss kills required for character Epoch changed from 10 -> 15 to add more unlock order consistency
- Unlocking character Epoch 4 requires defeating 15 total Elites instead of every Elite now
- Added a credits screen stub

- Upon opening the Timeline, if there are Epochs to reveal, center a random one

**WRITING:**

- All Ancient relic choices now show the associated relic's name
- Cleaned up **Drowning Beacon** event intro text
- Epochs with the exception of diary entries are now written in past tense

- Updated some Epoch text for the following characters:
  - Regent
  - Defect
  - Necrobinder
- Updated Epoch text for the following Ancients:
  - Orobas
  - Darv
- Fixed various localization errors

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Card highlights are now always consistent in Choose A Card screens
- Card type plaque now auto-expands when the text is too large
- Fixed profile deletion crash when directory contains hidden files
- Fixed an issue that made players unable to click the Exhaust button when peeking from a card selection
- Fixed an issue that made it possible to un-darken the card reward background with the spacebar
- Fixed not seeing accurate stats from final boss room in the run history
- Fixed skipping the tiny mailbox potion not showing up in your run history
- Fixed the event card count not showing up in run history
- Fixed the Merchant Robes relic blocking you from looking at your deck when you purchase the Orrery relic
- Fixed map icons appearing desaturated when they should be travelable
- Fixed an issue where players were unable to view stats when re-opening the card library

**Regent:**
- Fixed an issue where **Sovereign Blade** card's Forge achievement could not be earned if a later Regent achievement was already unlocked

**Potions & Relics:**
- Shop cards restocked by **The Courier** relic can no longer be on sale

**Ancients:**
- Fixed Ancient "Say Nothing" proceed prompt overlapping with the controller shortcut icon
- **Pael's Tooth** relic description no longer overflows the relic inspection screen
- The line "Is it even possible?" is now properly said by Necrobinder to Neow instead of vice versa
- The line "You can trust me" is now properly said by Tezcatara to Silent instead of vice versa
- Fixed incorrect speakers in Ironclad x Vakuu first visit dialogue

**Multiplayer:**
- Fur Coat relic now stacks correctly in multiplayer
- Ringing affliction now only applies to the player that played the card
- Vital Spark now triggers once per player in multiplayer

**PERFORMANCE:**

- FMOD music banks are now loaded per-act instead of all at once
- Only the selected music variant bank is loaded per act now
- VFX scenes are now asynchronously loaded one at a time instead of synchronously all at once
- Assets are now preloaded to reduce hitching in menus
- FMOD banks are now unloaded synchronously before loading new act banks

**MODDING:**

- Updated 0Harmony.dll with the MonoMod patch. Modders, please try using the built-in one again!
- Changed the modded warning to red if there is an error loading any assembly

Welcome to the first patch of February 2026! This one is more medium-sized, with a few balance and logic changes, a couple of reworks, and some art and UI additions.

**CONTENT:**

**Silent:**
- Added returning card from StS1, Expertise
- Deprecated Graceful card

**Necrobinder:**
- Reworked Countdown card

**Regent:**
- Reworked Black Hole card

**BALANCE:**

**Ironclad:**
- Changed **Grapple** card: now applies a debuff to the enemy instead of a buff to the player

**Defect:**
- Changed **Signal Boost** card: when played twice, it now doubles the next two Powers played, rather than increasing the doubling to tripling

**Necrobinder:**
- Osty now uses the player's block when he is dealt damage directly (for example, when using an Osty attack on an enemy with Thorns)
- Buffed **Bodyguard** card: Summon increased from 4(6) -> 5(7)
- Nerfed **Calcify** card: damage decreased from 5(7) -> 4(6)
- Changed **Friendship** card:
  - No longer lowers Dexterity
  - Strength loss increased from 1 -> 2(1)
  - No longer gains Innate on Upgrade
- Buffed **Unleash** card: base damage increased from 5(8) -> 6(9)

**Potions & Relics:**
- Changed **Vambrace** relic: now doubles Block gain from Melancholy card again
- Changed **Dragon Fruit** and **Planisphere** relics: no longer appear after the Act 3 treasure chest

**Enemies:**
- When playing **Hellraiser** card against the **Queen** boss, Strikes are now auto-played before Chains of Binding power afflicts them with Bound, meaning they no longer count as the Bound card played this turn
- Nerfed **Knowledge Demon**: Ponder move Strength gain decreased from 3(4) -> 2(3)
- Nerfed **Magi Knight**: Bomb move damage decreased from 40(45) -> 35(40)
- Buffed **Spectral Knight**:
  - Soul Slash move damage increased from 13(15) -> 15(17)
  - Now always does Soul Slash after Hex move

**Ancients:**
- Nerfed **Neow's Fury** card: no longer heals HP
- Changed Vakuu's **Potential** blessing:
  - Can no longer give 2 of the same curse
  - Can no longer give Greed curse

**Events:**
- Changed **Hungry for Mushrooms** event: relics now modify your HP by static amounts instead of percentage-based amounts (Big Mushroom gives you 20 max HP instead of 25% and Fragrant Mushroom makes you lose 15 HP instead of 20%)

**ART:**

- Added **Infested** Boss art (rename coming soon)
- Added portrait art for the following cards:
  - Bulk Up
  - Glasswork
  - Lightning Rod
  - Gunk Up
  - Helix Drill
  - Tesla Coil
- Changed **Dexterity Potion** asset color to green
- Added **Minion Dive Bomb** VFX
- Added **Underdocks** Rest Site background art and VFX
- Added **Underdocks** map asset
- Added new placeholder art for **Nonupeipe** and **Vakuu** Ancients

**AUDIO:**

- Added Dagger Spray SFX
- Added **Owl Magistrate** SFX

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

**General:**
- Added input remapping
- Added Cook Rest Site icon
- Updated patch notes icon to be high-res
- Added pause menu open/close SFX
- Added the ability to launch the game without mods
- Added support for European and Latin American Spanish
- The game now defaults to the current Steam UI language
- Added Power Saving Mode with paused banner
- Added controller support for map drawing
- Updated eraser tool hotspot so that it erases more cleanly
- When the Forge hovertip is shown, the Retain hovertip is now shown along with it

**Necrobinder:**
- **Eidolon** card now glows gold if you have 9+ other cards in your hand
- **Invoke** card now applies the Energy Next Turn power instead of the effect being part of the Invoke power

**Potions & Relics:**
- Relic icons are now only grayed out if their effect is used up for the entire run (for example, after Bone Tea relic upgrades your starting hand next combat)
- Added Cook tooltip to Meat Cleaver relic

**Enemies:**
- **Two-Tailed Rats** now spawn closer to the right side of the screen

**Ancients:**
- Ancient event choices now display the relic they will give you
- **Yummy Cookie** relic now switches which character it shows based on the local character

Multiplayer:
- Closing the map no longer removes your vote for the next node
- Hovering over cards in hand when it's not your turn now displays them fully
- Multiplayer vote icons now utilize an outline asset rather than a shader

**WRITING:**

- Renamed Forever Ice relic -> **Permafrost**
- Renamed Screamer Pod potion -> **Potion of Binding**
- **Astrolabe** and **New Leaf** relic descriptions now say "Transform cards" instead of "Choose and Transform cards"
- Effects that set a card's energy AND star costs to free now say "free to play" instead of "cost 0 "
- Fixed ordering of effects in **Convergence** card description

- More relics with "Upon pickup" effects now include "Upon pickup" in their descriptions
- **Orrery** relic description now says "Gain card rewards" instead of "Choose and add cards to your deck"
- **Reaper Form** and **Lethality** card wording changed to imply Osty attacks also benefit

**BUG FIXES:**

**General:**
- Players can now alt-enter from anywhere
- Card type label now auto-sizes to fit longer translations
- Fixed delay when game unpauses after regaining focus
- Fixed issues with game window only partially fullscreen-ing
- Fixed crash related to Korean text font
- Fixed an issue where cards' displayed types (Attack, Skill, etc.), some settings menu text, and the "View Upgrades" label in various card selection screens stayed in English after switching languages

**Ironclad:**
- When **Stomp** card is played after **Juggling** card, its clones now properly receive the cost reduction from itself being played

**Silent:**
- Moved Tough Bandages from shared relic pool to Silent's relic pool

**Necrobinder:**
- Fixed a visual issue with **Phylactery Unbound** relic where Osty wouldn't appear until after you'd drawn your opening hand
- **Melancholy** card now properly gains Block from **Pael's Legion** relic
- **Oblivion** card now properly triggers **Shroud** card

**Regent:**
- **Pillar of Creation** card is now properly triggered by **Severance** card
- Re-added missing **Gamma Blast** card art

**Colorless Cards:**
- **Entropy** card can no longer transform status cards into Disintegration, Mind Rot, Sloth, or Waste Away cards
- **Fisticuffs** card now properly includes overkill damage in its Block gain calculation

**Potions & Relics:**
- **Art of War** relic now properly disables Energy gain for the turn when an effect like Mayhem auto-plays an attack on turn start
- Added missing relic icons for **Looming Fruit** and **Sea Glass** relics
- **Hand Drill** relic now properly triggers when breaking an enemy's block with non-attack damage
- **Intimidating Helmet** relic no longer triggers when playing normally-2+ Energy cost cards whose costs have been reduced to 1 or 0 by global effects like Pounce
- **Tuning Fork** relic no longer triggers when **Mayhem** power autoplays an attack the turn after you've played 2 attacks

**Enemies:**
- Interrupting **Insatiable**'s Salivate move no longer makes sand VFX continuously play

**Ancients:**
- Vakuu's **Beauty** blessing now includes Eternal hovertip
- Nonupeipe's **Brilliant Scarf** relic no longer counts autoplayed cards towards the cards played this turn
- **Neow's Fury** card now shows up in the Compendium
- Neow's **Scroll Boxes** relic hovertip now properly mentions that the player loses all Gold upon pickup
- Treasure rooms no longer give the player Gold if they have Neow's **Silver Crucible** relic

**Events:**
- **Luminous Choir** event can no longer appear if the player can't afford the "Offer Tribute" option
- Selecting the Nap option at **Dense Vegetation** event no longer causes you to lose 5 max HP when you have the Night Terrors modifier
- Selecting the Nap option at **Dense Vegetation** event now properly triggers **Stone Humidifier** relic

**Multiplayer:**
- Players can now target allies with shared potions outside of combat
- **Oblivion** card no longer applies Doom for cards played by other players in multiplayer
- The run victory screen now works properly in multiplayer

**PERFORMANCE:**

- Compressed map assets
- Made various other performance improvements

Last week's patch was a bit on the smaller side, so to make up for it, this one is BIG. We've got a huge pass on enemy HP values and Ascension scaling, some new, reworked, returning, and deprecated content, UI/UX improvements, and a lot of changes to content unlocks.

Happy holidays! This is likely our last major patch of the year (unless we seriously broke stuff)

We have significant changes to the bosses: Doormaker and Knowledge Demon while also bringing in changes and tweaks for various content, notably for events.

You may notice that the game is slightly kinder during the holidays, as several relics have gotten their drop rules updated. Many cards now have updated names as we gear up the content for translation while ancient dialogues get a small clean up. The Timeline's getting some unlock changes soon... in preparation, there's bugfixes, cleanup, and simplification this patch.

In this patch we're welcoming a brand new Act 3 boss, The Doormaker, and saying goodbye to The Dreamer. You know what they say--when one door closes, another one opens! You'll also find BIG balance passes for the Necrobinder and the Defect (the latter of which has 4 shiny new cards!)

**BALANCE:**

General:
- Some effects that trigger via block or HP loss "from a card" now trigger "on your turn" instead (Beacon of Hope card, Rupture card, Laughing Stone relic)
- When a power applied by a card gives block (ex. After Image), deals damage (ex. Panache), or causes HP loss (ex. Inferno), the block/damage/HP loss is no longer considered to be "from a card".
- In combination with the new "from a card" -> "on your turn" change in various powers/relics, this should be a buff
- For example, one-off effects triggered by block gain (ex. Unmovable card) will no longer be accidentally consumed by automatic Block gain (ex. After Image card), while permanent effects triggered by Block gain (ex. Beacon of Hope) will be affected by new sources of Block gain (ex. Plating power)

In this September patch we're still churning our way through event updates and rewrites while polishing up art, SFX, and animations. Speaking of core content, Ceremonial Beast is reworked a bit again, now with a mix of new and old.

There's about ~50 runs being played per day right now, thank you all again for your playtest contributions and feedback :). We're still honing in on card balance and archetypes! Always open to feedback here--whether you're a seasoned veteran or starting off, all types of feedback and experiences are valid!

This week's patch brings a big content and balance pass, especially when it comes to The Necrobinder and The Regent. We're also saying goodbye to our old friend, the "Strength Down" power; whenever you would lose temporary Strength at the end of your turn you will now see a unique power based on the source of the temporary Strength. We're especially interested in getting feedback on how this change feels!

Another Thursday, another Byrdlett patch! This one brings a brand new Act 3 boss and a whole host of balance and UX changes. Many changes have been made to multiplayer specifically, so be sure to try out a multiplayer run if you haven't in a while! Also, the art additions that didn't make it into last week's patch have now actually been added.

All of this unlocking business revealed we needed some more content and so we're bringing in 17 relics and 4 potions. We're also adding some logic that makes it so you encounter each boss until you've seen them all.

There's also first time user experience stuff going in. Players will encounter a different boss each Act until you've fought them all. Afterwards, they will be randomized as usual. This feature exists in the first game as well, don't tell anyone. Similar logic exists for the very first run that you do, you'll always fight our simplest enemy on that run.

Necrobinder and Defect continue to get content and balance passes as we clean up some bugs from a lot of the combat logic cleanups which we're still bringing in.

Finally, there's a big speed up for card play as cards now kick off their effects while the card is flying towards the center upon playing (rather than after the card finishes flying to the center). Thanks to ocean for pointing it out :). Okay, have a nice week.

This week we see the biggest patch of all time (not really, but I figured it'd be nice to say so for Vex). We're shimming in a handful of features including Save Profiles and the long awaited Bestiary! The balance focus is on the Necrobinder this week while we incorporate UX polish and content related to the Timeline. Many enemies in the Underdocks have received sound effects and we also have a new track for the Test Subject boss.

It's time for the second June patch! This week brings in more controller support so you should be able to play an entire run from start to finish using a controller or Steam Deck. Still, it's quite early so feedback on its ux, ui, bugs, and general feedback about its implementation are appreciated. The support is in for Xbox controllers so other controllers may experience some weirdness or incompatibility at this time. Please report those as issues too!

Happy summer patch time!

This week Neow and Tanx are getting glow ups while we bring in a whole host of cards for everyone while focusing balance on the Underdocks.

A lot of the map assets have new colors, values, and some updated icons and we're also packing a lot of common textures so if you notice any strange visual issues or performance problems, do let us know because the texturepacker compatibility is a little janky. Things like image compression are now used on select assets as well so if you see non-placeholder assets with artifacting or anything, feedback there is appreciated.

A good chunk of core character SFX also made it in so each playable character should have more of its audio identity.

The Timeline is taking a time out this week as we want a few more UX touches before we take a look at it anew.

It's the last weekly patch... of May 2025 that is!
Okay okay, this week brings back the game mode: Custom.
It's called Custom Climb and is available for both Singleplayer and Multiplayer. Daily Climbs are now also available for Multiplayer. It'll be interesting to see how scores differ between a variety of group sizes.

Multiplayer also gets more interesting with the addition of cards exclusive for multiplayer!
These cards are sold by the Merchant, but depending on their popularity we may look into expanding how you get them and how much of this kind of content there is :).

We're deprecating a few Neow choices as we'll be bringing in changes here next week and potions are getting renames as their art assets will be getting a fresh look soon. Okay have fun.

It's the return of the Daily Climb this week! Those who have played at least one game can now participate in the Daily Climb. Does the leaderboard work? Do the modifiers cause issues? Have ideas for the daily?? Now's your chance to give feedback.

This patch also brings many changes to the Underdocks including 3 new encounters and lots of balancing and reworks while we continue to incorporate art and animations for these critters. There are also new Necrobinder cards and new Events which aren't exclusive to the Underdocks.

Upon winning or losing, there's now a more dramatic Climb Summary screen. You'll be able to speed it up later, but for now- patiently wait while viewing all the rooms you traversed and the amount of new things you discovered at the end of each climb.

This week, we're still making significant changes to the back end for flexibility and performance improvements so please let us know of any issues like broken textures or egregious hiccups. General feedback about overall performance or quality of certain assets is also appreciated.
Content is still focused on getting the Defect to a good place while making general balance adjustments.

At the start of each run, the player now goes straight to Neow and there's a less intense HP refill effect. This mimics STS1's behavior and saves a click.
The Timeline is functional again and while it still has no affect on gameplay, transitions are smoother, the requirements for obtaining them are no longer random, and some save migration logic has been added so the screen isn't too broken. It's not quite ready for primetime yet.

- Mad Crab's Hardened Shell power worded such that it cannot lose more than X amount of HP each turn

**BUG FIXES:**

Defect:
- Go for the Eyes card now works on Waterfall Giant's Explode move
- Particle Wall card no longer returns to the player's hand if some effect has added the Exhaust keyword to it
- Fixed an issue where Recycle card wasn't gaining extra energy when exhausting cards afflicted with Frozen

Necrobinder:
- Reaper Form card can no longer kill illusions like Parafright

Potions & Relics:
- Pandora's Box relic now flashes Egg relics over upgraded cards
- Heart of Iron potion now properly grants 5 Plating instead of 8

Ancients:
- Fixed an unexpected interaction between Enthralled curse card and effects that set a random card in your hand to free (such as Mummified Hand relic and Touch of Insanity potion). Previously, these effects would look for any card whose current energy cost was above 0, which could be due to a temporary cost-increasing effect like Enthralled. Now, these effects will prefer cards with a base energy cost above 0, and will only consider the modified cost if all cards in hand have a base energy cost of 0
- Fixed issue preventing Darv from giving energy relics in Act 3

Enemies:
- Ovicopter's eggs now spawn in with full HP
- Fixed Entomancer's animation interrupting itself

Events:
- Player no longer receives reward in The Legends Were True event if they die from choice damage

General:

- Cards that are currently being played no longer update their text
- Enemies' HP bars fade instead of disappear when they die
- Fixed issue where healthbar abruptly disappears when creatures die
- Intents can no longer change while they're being performed
- X-cost cards' energy cost text now remains white, even if they were generated by an effect like Discovery that modifies the card's cost

Multiplayer:
- Mad Crab's Hardened Shell power now scales up in multiplayer
- Fixed issue where players sometimes wouldn't see the game over screen
- You can no longer use potions while you are dead
- Mecha Knight no longer gives dead players Burn status cards

Got another patch for you all today and, because of PAX, it's another small one 🤏  Most of the content changes here are actually reworks of Tezcatara's and Zlatir's choices, which are in preparation for a new Ancient coming within the next couple weeks!

**CONTENT:**

Ironclad:
- Reworked Stomp card: “Deal 8 damage. hits an additional time if you've played at least 2 attacks this turn.”

Potions & Relics:
- Reworked Amethyst Bangle relic: “Card rewards can now contain colorless cards.”

Ancients:
- Removed Tezcatara’s Kindle options
- Reworked Tezcatara’s Ember choice: now only affects basic Strikes
- Reworked Vlad’s Wealth choice: “Gain 999 gold. All but 1 shop becomes a ? room”
- Reworked Zlatir’s Acknowledgement choice: no longer adds Scorned to cards
- Reworked Zlatir’s Immortal choice: now gives you Cursed Blood (gain 50% less block) instead of 4 Decay
- Reworked Zlatir’s Power choice: gives you 1 Folly (while in hand, take 1 damage whenever you play a card) instead of 3 Normalities

**BALANCE:**

Regent:
- Nerfed Parry card: no longer Forges

Colorless Cards:
- Changed Anointed card: upgrade changed from Innate -> Retain

Enemies:
- Changed Furry Worm Crawler:
 - Damage decreased from 5 -> 4
 - Strength gain increased from 5 -> 7
- Changed Nibbit:
 - Butt damage increased from 13 -> 14
 - Slice damage and Block increased from 6 -> 7
- Buffed Shrinker Beetle: Stomp damage increased from 15 -> 16
- Nerfed The Legend:
 - No longer gains Block or Plating on its Buff turn
 - No longer starts with Plating
 - Weak applied on its Beat Up turn decreased from 2 -> 1
 - Sealed Sword’s Herald of Tempests card's starting damage increased from 1 -> 11

**ART:**

- Added Tezcatara animations
- Added playtester beta art from the Byrdlett server
- Added Molten Fist card VFX
- Added colored card trails for Silent, Defect, Regent, Necrobinder

**USER INTERFACE & EXPERIENCE:**

- Added the keyword Eternal (cannot be removed from your deck)
- Ethereal hovertip now also always comes with Exhaust hovertip
- Overhauled topbar node properties to prevent text shrinkage and overlap
- Resized map drawing buttons a bit
- The dev console can now be opened by the ' key to better support Swedish keyboards

**WORDING:**

- Reworded Zlatir’s Beauty choice
- Updated Stomp card’s wording to make it more clear how much damage you are doing

**BUG FIXES:**

- Photon Blade card now has correct card selection prompt
- Fixed issue preventing you from previewing cards in deck view
- Fixed softlock on power swoosh
- Selecting cards in hand correctly cancels queued card plays
- Strength from a previous combat no longer affects upgrade previews in rest sites

It's Byrdlett Build... *checks notes*... Thursday?! That's right, going forward we'll be releasing new builds on Thursdays instead of Fridays, that way we have some more leeway if we run into any obstacles and we can more easily get hotfixes out if needed. We've got a relatively small one today (no new Content™️ this time) but there's some balance changes and art/animation additions for y'all to check out!

**BALANCE:**

Regent:
- Changed Monologue card: upgrade changed from Strength increase to giving it Retain
- Reworked Prophesize card:
 - Cost changed from 0 Energy 2 Stars -> 2 Energy 0 Stars
 - Card draw increased from 3(4) -> 5(8)
- Changed Summon Forth card:
 - No longer Exhausts
 - Cost increased from 0 Energy -> 1 Energy

Colorless Cards:
- Changed The Gambit card:
 - No longer a Silent card, now a Colorless card
 - Block gain increased from 16(24) -> 50(75)

Enemies:

 - Damage ramp increased from 4 Strength -> 5

**ART:**

- Added VFX for Dramatic Entrance card

- Updated map drawing assets
- Added Queen and Torchead Amalgam Animations

**USER EXPERIENCE & INTERFACE:**

- Added energy icon to hovertip
- Allowed card play via Click to Target (not just drag to target)
- Added various debug commands to toggle certain visuals on/off
- Updated debug info font to be semibold + reduced spacing, font size, and transparency

**WORDING:**

- Fixed spacing on Panic Button and Hand of Greed cards
- Shortened Ethereal tooltip
- Fixed Wellspring event typo

**BUG FIXES:**

Ironclad:
- Armaments card upgrade previews now accounts for player powers
- Dampen now resets Searing Blow to correct amount of upgrades on removal

Silent:
- You can no longer use the Well Laid Plans card to retain a Toxic status card

Regent:

Misc:

We're back to our regularly scheduled programming this week--another Slay the Spire 2 build is here! In terms of content for this patch we have two new Act 1 bosses and some new Regent cards along with lots of balance and UI/UX updates. And in even bigger news, **we're switching to Steam builds from here on out!**

**IMPORTANT: this means you will have to mark the game as private on Steam so that StS2 and StS2-related activity is hidden on your profile like so:**
**- Activate the game key in Steam**
**- Right-click on Slay the Spire 2 in your library**
**- Click Manage -> Mark as Private**

**We've attached a video demonstrating the process, too. Just DM/ping Casey or Anthony for a key and they'll get you one!**

- Added Lunar Blast card
- Added Orbital Shield card

- Added Vantom, a new Act 1 boss
- Added The Kin, a new Act 1 boss

<@&1070463562008510616> Another Friday, another Byrdlett build! This one comes with some big Vlad--or should we say Zlatir--changes among various bug fixes, UI & UX additions, and balance changes!

**Heads up that we made some changes to our save file format which require your run history to be reset!** If you have an in-progress run, the game will auto-abandon it. So if you have an in-progress run you want to finish, you should finish it on your current build before downloading the new one.

Here are the steps to deleting your run history:

1. Open the command line with ~ key
2. Type "open saves"
3. Delete the settings.save, the progress.save, and the run_history folder

- Fixed screenshake intensity affecting duration (though it’s not configurable at this time)
- Fixed softlock when trying to transform an unremovable card.
- Increased room scale no longer offsets intent positions for spawned creatures mid-combat
- Lightning orb sparks no longer render over map
- Shop items are no longer selected if you release mouse while not hovering over it
- Rewards screen now waits for enemy death animations again
- Run History now accurately shows how much damage you took in a fight (regardless of if you healed)
- Run History correctly reflects won runs again (old runs will remain incorrect)
- Run History no longer viewable if you haven't done any runs
- Run History rooms should no longer be off by one after Act 1 (this may break some older run histories)
The correct floor number should now display correctly after Act 1
- The "Dead" text on a killed creature's health bar is no longer tinted purple
- The Abandon and Save run buttons no longer leak memory
- The game no longer waits until the beginning of the player's turn to finish combat, which fixes Book of Five Rings flashing right after Poison kills the last enemy
- Victory and Abandon quotes no longer change between views of a run's history

**PERFORMANCE:**

- Fixed a memory leak that occurred between runs
- Improved FPS by preloading new assets

Hey playtesters, got another patch for ya! And it's a doozy. First of all... THE NECROBINDER REWORK IS HERE!! No more swapping! Necro's HP is now a fraction of what it was but, at the end of combat, she heals completely AND revives Osty if he's dead! Another big change to her kit is she can now sacrifice her own HP to boost Osty in various ways through cards. We're very interested in hearing your thoughts on her new playstyle!! 💀 ✨ 

Other notable changes are that this is the first Godot build with music and SFX (wooo!) and the Regent's Gravity mechanic has started to be phased out (still a WIP!)

Also, one of the bugs we've been tracking is a particular kind of softlock. We've seen it a couple of times but it's particularly tricky to reproduce. Unlike other softlocks, the common symptom of this one:
- you can't hover or /play cards in your hand (even for tooltips).
- you can still technically hit End Turn and the combat progresses (though obviously you are stuck because you can't play cards)

So far we're calling this bug the hand lock (or hand cramp). If you see it, please file it as such with as much info as you can. Happy slaying!

Also the Regent is looking SO SNAZZY with his new animations, assets, and card art!! Revel in our special boy's glory ✨ 

By the way, some things we have planned changes for are Shattered, Byrdonis, and Gravity, so don't worry about those too much for now. In the meantime, let's dig into this!

The Necrobinder and her faithful right-hand left hand are BACK and totally revamped! She now has 3 brand new cards under her belt and 2 new powers, Shield and Vigor. You may notice that many of her cards have been reworked, her and Osty’s HP has been rebalanced, Osty must now be revived/healed at rest sites, AND you can now freely switch between which character is in front. We’d greatly appreciate your feedback on anything Necrobinder-related! ☠️ 

We’ve also got tons of updated event art, UI changes, and bug fixes, among other things. Let’s dig in!

**CONTENT:**

- Added Shield power
   - “The next time you gain Block from a card, gain X additional Block.”
- Added Vigor power
   - “Your next Attack deals X additional damage.”
- "Born to Die" card has been removed, and "Deathbringer" card has been added
   - Type: Common Attack
 - Energy cost: 2
 - "Deal 13(17) damage to ALL enemies."
- Added "Drain Life" Necrobinder card
   - Type: Uncommon Attack
  - Energy cost: 1
 - "Deal 7(9) damage. Gain Vigor equal to unblocked damage dealt."
- Added "Grave Warden" Necrobinder card
   - Type: Common Skill
 - Energy cost: 1
 - You and Osty each gain 5(8) Shield.
- Better Than Ever card has been reworked
   - Rarity: Rare -> Uncommon
 - "If Osty is dead, revive him and apply 5(8) Strength." -> "Transfer all your Vigor to Osty. Draw 1 card."
 - Upgrade: Adds Retain keyword
- Bodyguard card has been reworked
   - Type: Power -> Skill
 - "Osty gains 3(4) Dexterity." -> "Osty gains 12(16) Block and moves to the front. You can't swap this turn."
- Loyalty card has been reworked
   - Type: Attack -> Skill
 - "If Osty is dead, deal 11(16) damage." -> "You and Osty each gain 3(4) Vigor."
- Shared Fate card has been reworked
   - "For each Curse obtained this run, apply 6(9) Doom to ALL enemies." -> "Lose 3(4) Strength. Enemy loses 3(4) Strength."
- Heel card has been reworked
   - "Osty gains 15(20) Block." -> "Osty gains 8(11) Shield."
- Dark Siphon card has been reworked
    - Type: Skill -> Attack
 - Energy cost increased from 1 -> 2
 - "Osty heals HP equal to the amount of Doom on ALL enemies." -> "Osty deals 18(24) damage. If this kills an enemy, gain 3 energy. Exhaust."
- Flatten card has been reworked
   - "Osty attacks 5 times." ->  "Osty deals 5(6) damage 5 times."
- Plague Carrier card has been reworked
   - "Whenever Osty dies, ALL enemies gain 20(25) Doom." -> "Whenever Osty gains Block, apply 3(4) Doom to a random enemy."

**BALANCE:**

- Decreased Necrobinder's HP from 42 -> 35
- Increased Osty's HP from 15 -> 45
- Enemy debuffs are now redirected to Osty if he's in front
- Osty no longer revives and heals at the start of every room. Instead, Osty can be revived and healed by resting at rest sites
- Fetch card type changed from Skill -> Attack
- Reanimate card cost increased from 0 -> 1. Upgrading Reanimate now reduces its cost to 0
- Sacrifice card energy gain increased from 3(4) ->  4(5). Rarity changed from Uncommon -> Rare

**BUG FIXES:**

- Fearmonger power now triggers when "Osty deals damage" attacks are played
- Brainbug card selection now fades out to not overlap with relic bar
- Fixed the ability to deselect cards from Gambling Chip
- Energy orb shiny UI can no longer be seen in Neow room

- Fixed character layering issues

**METRICS:**

**ART:**

- Updated event art for the following 16 events: Battleworn Dummy, Brain Bug, Bug Slayer, Buried Seed, Byrdonis Egg, Cursed Choice, Field of the Forgotten, Hungry for Mushrooms, Infested Automaton, Self-Help Book, Symbiote, Telepathic Spiders, The Future of Potions, The Trial, Tinker Time, Wood Carvings

- Updated potion assets

**UI & EFFECTS:**

- “Abandon Run” confirmation popup buttons are now larger
- Added hide/show logic for all combat HUD toggler components
- Added button for swapping between Necrobinder and Osty being "in front"
- Adjusted common button outlines to be thinner and always displayed for now
